description: Compute the expected return of a portfolio as the weighted average of the expected returns on its component securities using currency-weighted portfolio weights

fields.name: holdings
fields.label: Array of {asset_id, weight, expected_return} for each portfolio position

weight_properties.negative_weights_allowed: Short positions produce w_i < 0; long-short portfolios still require sum(w_i) = 1

interpretation.weighted_average: Portfolio expected return is a linear combination of asset expected returns — no diversification effect on return (unlike variance)
interpretation.forward_looking: E(R_i) are forecast means, not historical sample means

applications.performance_attribution: Decompose realized return into weight contributions
applications.benchmarking: Compare active portfolio E(Rp) vs benchmark E(Rp)

validation.non_empty: holdings array must contain at least one asset
validation.returns_numeric: All expected_return values must be numeric

compute_expected_return.description: Weighted average of component expected returns

errors.message: Holdings array must not be empty
Portfolio weights must sum to 1 within tolerance

anti_patterns.rule: Do not compute portfolio expected return from realised asset returns in a single period
anti_patterns.why: E(R_i) are forecast means, not single-period outcomes; using realised returns conflates expected value with a noisy sample draw

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use equal weights when market-value weights are available
anti_patterns.why: Market-cap weighted portfolios (e.g. benchmarks) must reflect currency proportions; equal weighting systematically tilts toward small caps

anti_patterns.rule: Do not omit short positions by treating them as zero weights
anti_patterns.why: Short weights are negative contributions to E(Rp); zeroing them overstates long-only return and misrepresents the portfolio

anti_patterns.rule: Do not mix percentage and decimal return inputs in the same calculation
anti_patterns.why: 13% as 13 vs 0.13 produces 100x errors; normalise inputs to a single convention before aggregation
